name: activate-task-before-work
tags: task, set_active, activate, workflow, task-framework, context, checkpoint
body:
Always call tasks__set_active before touching any file or reading code for a task — not after.

**Why:** Activating a task does two things: (1) it transitions status open→active so tasks__finish works cleanly at the end without DB workarounds, and (2) the LangGraph checkpoint starts injecting task context (body, decisions, history, related tasks, related commits) into every subsequent turn. That context makes the work better — Claude sees the plan, prior decisions, and related code chunks automatically. Skipping activation means working blind and then hitting gate errors at close time.

**How to apply:** The moment a task is identified for the current session, call tasks__set_active first — before reading files, before planning, before any edits. For multi-subtask work, activate each subtask in turn as you move to it.
